ddls:
CREATE TABLE "companies" (
  "company_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each company */,
  "company_name" TEXT /* Name of the company */,
  "sector_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sector in which the company operates */,
  "industry_id" INTEGER /* ID of the industry classification of the company */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* ID of the location of the company's headquarters */,
  "rank" INTEGER /* Ranking of the company in the Fortune Global 500 */,
  "founded_year" INTEGER /* Year the company was founded */,
  "ceo_name" TEXT /* Name of the current CEO */,
  "ceo_start_date" TEXT /* Date the current CEO started */,
  "website" TEXT /* Company's official website URL */,
  "stock_ticker" TEXT /* Stock ticker symbol */,
  "market_cap" REAL /* Market capitalization */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the company's data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("company_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_companies_sector_id FOREIGN KEY ("sector_id") REFERENCES sectors ("sector_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_companies_industry_id FOREIGN KEY ("industry_id") REFERENCES industries ("industry_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_companies_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES locations ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "financials" (
  "financial_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each financial record */,
  "company_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the company */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of the financial data */,
  "quarter" INTEGER /* Quarter of the financial data */,
  "revenue" REAL /* Total revenue of the company */,
  "profits" REAL /* Profits of the company */,
  "employees" INTEGER /* Number of employees */,
  "assets" REAL /* Total assets */,
  "liabilities" REAL /* Total liabilities */,
  "equity" REAL /* Shareholder equity */,
  "cash_flow" REAL /* Operating cash flow */,
  "debt" REAL /* Total debt */,
  "ebitda" REAL /* Earnings Before Interest, Taxes, Depreciation, and Amortization */,
  "roe" REAL /* Return on Equity */,
  "roa" REAL /* Return on Assets */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("financial_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_financials_company_id FOREIGN KEY ("company_id") REFERENCES companies ("company_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sectors" (
  "sector_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sector */,
  "sector_name" TEXT /* Name of the sector */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the sector */,
  "parent_sector_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent sector (if applicable) */,
  "market_size" REAL /* Estimated market size of the sector */,
  "growth_rate" REAL /* Annual growth rate of the sector */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sector_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sectors_parent_sector_id FOREIGN KEY ("parent_sector_id") REFERENCES sectors ("sector_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "industries" (
  "industry_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each industry */,
  "industry_name" TEXT /* Name of the industry */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the industry */,
  "parent_industry_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent industry (if applicable) */,
  "market_size" REAL /* Estimated market size of the industry */,
  "growth_rate" REAL /* Annual growth rate of the industry */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("industry_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_industries_parent_industry_id FOREIGN KEY ("parent_industry_id") REFERENCES industries ("industry_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "locations" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each location */,
  "location_name" TEXT /* Name of the location */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country in which the location is situated */,
  "city" TEXT /* City where the company's headquarters is located */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the location */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the location */,
  "timezone" TEXT /* Timezone of the location */,
  "region" TEXT /* Region (e.g., North America, Europe) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user in the EPMS */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for security */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last login */,
  "status" TEXT /* User status (active, inactive, suspended) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of user creation */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to user data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)
column_names: ID of the sector in which the company operates
ID of the industry classification of the company
ID of the location of the company's headquarters
Ranking of the company in the Fortune Global 500
Timestamp of the last update to the company's data

ID of the parent sector (if applicable)
